description_long: This object consists of a group of four rustic vases and bowls, each with a distinct yet harmonious form, arranged closely together. The surfaces are textured and mottled, featuring a blend of earthy terracotta and greenish moss-like patina, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The largest vessel is a tall, wide-mouthed vase with a rounded body and a slightly flared rim, while the others include a smaller bottle-shaped vase and two bowl-like containers, one of which contains a pestle. The vessels have thick walls and a hand-crafted appearance, with visible irregularities and roughness in their finish. The coloration and weathered look evoke traditional pottery often found in outdoor urban gardens, courtyards, or historical parks. The set appears robust and functional, suitable for decorative or utilitarian use in public or communal spaces.
description: A weathered set of four rustic terracotta vases and bowls with earthy tones and mossy patina, featuring rough, hand-crafted surfaces and varied forms. The vessels evoke traditional pottery, ideal for outdoor urban decoration.
description_view_0: The largest vase dominates the view, with the smaller bowl and bottle-shaped vase partially visible behind it, highlighting the textured, mossy surface.
description_view_1: All four vessels are clearly visible, with the bowl and pestle in the foreground and the tallest vase at the back, showcasing the group's arrangement and size differences.
description_view_2: The tall vase is prominent, with the smaller vases and bowl clustered in front, emphasizing the earthy coloration and rough finish.
description_view_3: The view focuses on the rear of the largest vase, with the other vessels partially obscured, drawing attention to the patina and rounded forms.
